# @zakkster/lite-headless/kanban

Headless kanban board: columns of cards with drag-to-reorder (intra-column)
and programmatic + HTML5-DnD-based moves between columns.

Composes `createSortable` per column for the in-column reorder.

## API

```js
import { createKanban } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/kanban";

const kb = createKanban({
    columns: [
        { id: "todo",  title: "To Do" },
        { id: "doing", title: "Doing" },
        { id: "done",  title: "Done" },
    ],
    cards: [
        { id: "c1", columnId: "todo", title: "Buy milk" },
        { id: "c2", columnId: "todo", title: "Write blog post" },
    ],
    onCardMove: (cardId, fromColumnId, toColumnId, newIndex, reason) => {},
    onCardClick: (cardId, domEvent) => {},
    sortableOptions: { /* forwarded to each column's sortable */ },
});
```

## Shapes

```js
// ColumnDef
{ id: string, title: string, ...meta }

// CardDef
{ id: string, columnId: string, title: string, ...meta }
```

`id` is required and must be unique. `meta` fields are preserved
verbatim — consumers can stash arbitrary data (priority, assignees,
labels, etc.).

## Reactive accessors

```js
kb.columns()                 // ColumnDef[]
kb.cards()                   // CardDef[]
kb.cardsInColumnReactive(id) // Card[] in display order (reactive inside an effect)
```

## Queries (non-reactive)

```js
kb.cardsInColumn(colId)   // Card[] in display order
kb.getCard(id)            // Card | null
kb.getColumn(id)          // Column | null
```

## Mutations

```js
kb.addColumn(col)             // dedup'd by id
kb.removeColumn(id)           // removes column + all its cards
kb.addCard(card)              // dedup'd by id; rejects unknown columnId
kb.removeCard(id)
kb.updateCard(id, partial)    // columnId field cannot be changed here (use moveCard)
kb.moveCard(cardId, toColumnId, toIndex?, reason?)
```

`moveCard` is the only API for changing a card's `columnId`. The
`reason` argument is forwarded to `onCardMove` (defaults to `"api"`).

## Attach methods

```js
kb.attachColumn(el, colId)           // wires column container as sortable root
kb.attachCard(el, cardId)            // wires card into its column's sortable
kb.attachHandle(cardEl, handleEl)    // limits drag origin to a handle within a card
kb.attachDraggable(cardEl, cardId)   // optional: HTML5-DnD draggable=true setup
kb.attachDropZone(columnEl, colId)   // optional: HTML5-DnD drop target for cross-column moves
```

`attachCard` must be called AFTER `attachColumn` for the same column —
the card hooks into the column's sortable instance.

## Cross-column drag-and-drop

Three ways to wire cross-column moves:

### A. Programmatic only

```js
kb.moveCard("c1", "doing", 0);
```

The simplest case. Useful for bulk operations, undo, or when DnD is
driven by something other than HTML5 (touch gestures, voice command,
etc.).

### B. Built-in HTML5 DnD (zero extra wiring)

For each column container, call `attachDropZone`. For each card, call
`attachDraggable`. The wrapper variant does both automatically when
the host has the `html5-dnd` attribute.

```js
const cards = document.querySelectorAll(".card");
for (const card of cards) {
    kb.attachDraggable(card, card.dataset.cardId);
}
const columns = document.querySelectorAll(".column");
for (const col of columns) {
    kb.attachDropZone(col, col.dataset.columnId);
}
```

The HTML5 DnD path writes the card id into
`dataTransfer.setData("text/x-kanban-card-id", cardId)`. The drop
handler parses it back, computes the target index from the pointer y
coordinate (vs the midpoints of cards in the target column), and
calls `moveCard`.

### C. Pointer-based DnD (consumer-built)

If you want unified pointer-based drag for both intra- and inter-
column (with touch + scroll handling), build it on top of the
`moveCard` API. The primitive doesn't ship its own pointer-based
cross-column drag because in-column pointer drag is already provided
by sortable, and mixing modes leads to UX bugs.

## Painted attributes

### Columns (via attachColumn → sortable.attachRoot)
- `role="listbox"` (sortable's choice; kanban accepts the listbox
  semantics because cards are keyboard-focusable + pickable)
- `data-kanban-column-id="<colId>"`
- (HTML5 DnD only) `data-kanban-drop-active` while pointer is over

### Cards (via attachCard → sortable.attachItem)
- `role="option"`
- `tabindex="0"`
- `data-kanban-card-id="<cardId>"`
- `data-kanban-card-column="<colId>"` (updated when the card moves)
- (HTML5 DnD only) `data-kanban-card-dragging` while being dragged

## Custom element

```html
<lite-kanban html5-dnd>
    <div data-kanban-column="todo" data-kanban-column-title="To Do">
        <div data-kanban-card-id="c1">Buy milk</div>
        <div data-kanban-card-id="c2">Write blog post</div>
    </div>
    <div data-kanban-column="doing" data-kanban-column-title="Doing">
        <div data-kanban-card-id="c3">Code review</div>
    </div>
    <div data-kanban-column="done" data-kanban-column-title="Done">
    </div>
</lite-kanban>
```

The wrapper auto-discovers columns + cards from the markup. Adding a
`data-kanban-column-title="..."` populates the `title` field of the
auto-created column. Adding `html5-dnd` to the host enables built-in
HTML5 drag-and-drop wiring (otherwise the wrapper only wires in-column
sortable reorder).

### Custom element host API

```js
host.columns             // ColumnDef[] (accessor)
host.cards               // CardDef[] (accessor)
host.cardsInColumn(id)
host.getCard(id) / .getColumn(id)
host.moveCard(cardId, toColumnId, toIndex, reason?)
host.addCard(card) / .removeCard(id) / .updateCard(id, partial)
host.addColumn(col) / .removeColumn(id)
host._kanbanInstance     // raw primitive escape hatch
```

### Custom element events

- `cardmove`  `{ detail: { cardId, fromColumnId, toColumnId, newIndex, reason } }`
- `cardclick` `{ detail: { cardId } }`

## Composition

- **`lite-popover`**: click a card to open an edit popover.
- **`lite-dialog`**: open a "new task" modal that calls
  `host.addCard(...)` on submit.
- **`lite-toast`**: feedback on card moves ("Moved to Done").
- **`lite-skeleton`**: skeleton column while tasks load from server.
- **`lite-inline-edit`**: edit a card title inline without opening a
  modal.

## Notes

- The primitive does NOT pre-build any DOM. Consumers provide their own
  column containers + card elements; the primitive paints attributes
  and wires sortable.
- In-column reorder is handled by sortable's pointer events (touch +
  mouse). Keyboard pick-up (Space to grab, arrows to move, Space to
  drop) is also provided by sortable.
- Cross-column reorder via HTML5 DnD has known mobile limitations
  (touch support is patchy across browsers). For touch-first apps,
  build cross-column drag on top of `moveCard` with pointer events.
- `removeColumn` removes the column AND all cards in it. To "archive"
  cards instead, call `removeColumn` and then `addCard` to recreate
  them in a different column.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
